free_text: The 45Sc(a,p)48Ti and 45Sc(a,n)48V cross sections
5U Pelletron accelerator
Three metallic targets of Sc were prepared, two on
gold backings of stopping thickness (1.91+-0.12)x10**18
and (4.69+-0.28)x10**18/cm2 for 10 MeV alpha-particles
and the other a self-supporting  transmission target.
Transmission target was prepared by evaporation of Sc
metal on to a glass slide on to which thin layer of
99.999% pure caesium bromide had been evaporated.
Convensional transmission detector/surface
barrier detector telescope mounted 5.8cm from the
target in 123 degree direction, which corresponded to
125 degree c.m. The transmission detector was thick
enough to stop 10 MeV alpha-particles but thin enough
to transmit all protons for which statistical-model
calculations predicted a significant yield.
Gamma-ray excitation function was measured with
a 125 cm3 Ge(Li) detector located 3.5 cm from the
target in the 55 degr.

Fig.4
Detector consisted of a 32x32x44 cm3 paraffin
block in which six 3He-filled proportional counters
were mounted parallel to the beam direction, the
outputs of which were summed.
Gamma rays were detected with a 125 cm3 Ge(Li)
detector located 2.7 cm from  the target in the 0 degr.

The absolute error in the cross-section scale
for the activation measurement.
The absolute error in the cross-section scale
for the neutron counting measurement.
The main sources of error were detection efficiency,
7% for Ge detector and 12% for the neutron detector,
target thickness 6%, and beam current integration 5%.
